tokens: And, Charlie, (we'd like the) DAC, On, as you start driving.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We're in and we're on our way.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Station 6 will be completely nominal.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Why don't we just make it 3.5 at 2.9.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I want to follow my tracks down this thing.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, we're riding at idle (meaning that he is not applying any power to the wheels), and she's picking up speed.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we would like Station 6 at the lowest terrace on Stone Mountain and a blocky crater, if possible.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The lowest terrace (and) a blocky crater.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Charlie, where do you think Stone Mountain takes off in the...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I can see a place down here.
See that...Off to the left, John, by where your tracks came up there....
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...There's a pretty big crater, with some blocks around it.
(Pause) Okay, Tony, where do you estimate one would be?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now, there's one right down there, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You'll be in the neighborhood and range (at) about 3.0 or a little bit greater.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm just glad that we don't have that watch-the-Rover-go TV.
(Laughing) Because I don't think we'd be going.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Just to the left there, John.
See those blocks over there in that crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, on over farther than that.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You want to take off and go that way now?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Right on the other side of this crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Kind of expect trouble from backseat drivers.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Why don't we go down a little bit further.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Turns out it's not a crater at all anyway.
(Pause) There are some interesting angular blocks here.
It's still apparently...Well, we're steering (on a heading of) about 340 now, and we're getting some angular blocks and cobbles just as we described before.
20 percent of the surface (is covered), perhaps.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The nominal Station 4 (means Station 6) would be on a bearing of 356 (based on the known LM location).
(Pause) But you're going to have to use...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We'll veer over that...(Stops to listen) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Tony, over on Smoky (Mountain), it doesn't appear...I can see the lineations over there, but they're more widely spaced than (what Charlie saw) on Stone here (from the LM); and (they're) mostly parallel to the Cayley (meaning that the lineations are horizontal).
(To John) Bear...Bear left a little bit, John, if you can.
We can start looking for a place now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Here's the bench right down here, I think.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now if we drive along the bench, maybe we'll find a blocky crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Want to go right or left?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Because then Station 8's on over to the left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Look at that block there, Charlie!
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That big block right over there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Driving west, along a bench, trying to find a blocky crater.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Passing these big glass-splattered rocks that apparently came out of South Ray.
(I) think every one of them did, frankly.
(Pause) Hey, here's a crater here, John, that's about 10 meters (across), that (has a) fairly blocky rim, angular.
It's a secondary on this side.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Tony, it's a secondary (that was dug by a large piece of South Ray ejecta).
We're at 357 and 3.1, and we got a secondary that's fairly blocky rimmed.
There's one up just a little upslope from us that might be better.
Though there's not as many blocks on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; we'd like the more blocky one.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I think it's a secondary.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, you park a 180 here, John (as indicated on
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: If I park 180, we'll be downslope from it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No, the crater's over to the left.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I know where the crater is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Why, don't you just park north?
I can point that (high-gain) antenna north.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You ought to get over here to the right a little bit so the TV won't be looking into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Now I'll just swing around and point it north.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay; we copy that, and verify DAC off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And we'll also need EMU checks here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I don't have any flags; I can't see my quantity.
Sixty-five percent and (pause) my pressure's good, and I haven't changed my
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, I didn't...We didn't sink near...Of course, we're not standing on the rim of a crater.
But this is harder, because we're just sort of bouncing here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, it's a lot more...Tony, the regolith character, as John said, has really changed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: When we walk, we don't bounce as much...(correcting himself) I mean, we don't sink in as much.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And you hardly got any dust on you, but I'm going to do you a favor and dust you off.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Trying to see) what this rock's made out of.
I'm trying to get a fresh surface.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Dusting the TV lens with the big brush) Let me help you there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ah, thank you much there, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That matrix there's pure white, John, with black phenocryst in it.
And it's got some lath-like crystals in it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's the one I'm talking about.
See, I just broke that open.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let me get a bigger piece of it, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I've whacked about five times, as hard as I could.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Houston, it has a white matrix with square clasts and elongate clasts in it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That might be...And it's rounded, too.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Very good, and we understand it's a two-rock breccia.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Looks like some more of them right down here, John, that are...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let's get some of those.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, John, we'll need an EMU check.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Every time I read my oxygen gauge, (laughing) I get an earful of orange juice.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Grab the gnomon, John, and we...How about a set of tongs and a shovel?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that'll probably save us some energy.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yep; think we might need it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, you got any recommendations there?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I think you ought to just use your scoop or something for a gnomon.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Pulled it right out of there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: ...but I understand exactly what you mean.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I got the wand, and the legs stayed in the bag.
Okay, give me a shovel, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; well, we can use the shovel if...Where do you want to go?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This looks like a...Here's a nice little cracked one right there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There's some angular and rounded rocks right there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Just pick anything in the picture for scale...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: You can stick the tongs in there, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I tried that a minute ago.
I don't see why I'm doing a down-Sun (pause) but I'll do it.

tokens: Okay, Tony; can you get a locator from the TV on this sample?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Tony, not having heard the response) Just pan by it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (As Charlie positions the scoop to pour) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, that's going in bag 407.
It was some soil and some dirt, a rounded rock...Wait a minute, Charlie; let me get an "after" of that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let's grab this angular one next.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Petulantly) Let me get it with the tongs.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Rising with the rock) Leaning on that shovel is easy.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Might be the same kind, John, that you picked up the way there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That one won't get sacked there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Why don't you get a soil sample?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and you've got about 10 minutes left before we'll have to leave.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay; maybe we could go to one more area.
And so we won't salt it with...This is just one broken-up block here.

tokens: Hey, you fellows are always thinking.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That spinning up that bag really works great.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: See anything down under there, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is a second(ary)...This is rim of it.
I didn't have any trouble digging down with the shovel.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Solid gray all the way down.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me put this (soil sample) in your bag.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, how about on the upper rim there?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This might have been a secondary.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What's that right over there, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's a really a unique white-looking something-or-other.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, you want to get some of it?
It's unusual soil, if it is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It might have been just a little...Yeah, it looks like a little teeny impact, doesn't it?
(Pause) Hey, let's get a quick one and then go on up here and get some of these blocks on the upper rim.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Run around and get a locator.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, this one-sixth gravity is so neat!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Got you a bag coming, John.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, what we're picking up is a white...It looks like a little patch of indurated regolith, and it's whitish in color.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let's get a little bit more of the white, John.
That got most of it; it was just on the end of the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, that soil sample's in 339, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And I'm through frame count 106, now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Maybe we can get a chance to get up another sample.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, you've got about time for just one more.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I been hankering for a piece off that rock.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Here's an old rounded one right here, John.
With the white spots in it.
I don't see...(Pointing left) Here's a real angular one right over here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Let's get this rounded one, Charlie...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And it's so dust-covered that I'm unable to describe it, although it's a white subrounded rock.
And I can't distinguish any crystalline structure in it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay; and you've got 5 minutes here.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Charlie, put that one in mine.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got to change out bags.
(Getting in position to stow the sample) Whoops!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah; you fellows have been really scooping them up there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Bring me the scoop a minute.
Let me whack this thing right here.
It's so tempting I can't pass it up.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: All right, there's a good place to whack.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Standing) I'm sorry we didn't get it documented before, Tony, but that is a good sample.
It's a...I think it's a crystalline rock.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, let's go ahead and document it now.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...We'll get the location of the one that's still in place (meaning the parent rock).
It didn't look like it moved.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No, he didn't move anything there.
I'm gonna do an up-Sun on this documentation.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'll get a cross-Sun here.
It's a grayish-bluish (pause) rock, Tony, in the matrix with some white clast in it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Petulantly) Oh, Ch(arlie)...Let me get it with the (tongs)...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, the matrix is so fine-grained, I can't tell, Tony; but it's definitely got a blue cast to it and there are inclusions of a whitish...It looked like plage to me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're gonna have to move out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, ah, needle-like, black crystals in it, too.

tokens: Here's another piece (that) came off the same rock.

tokens: It has this white clast in it.

tokens: Boy, is this a neat way to travel!

tokens: Okay; we're going mode switch to 1.

transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, it was on the floor.

tokens: You want me to get out and help you?

tokens: Dusty; we'll dust it at the next place.

tokens: And, Charlie, we'll need a frame count.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'm sorry it's taking me...(Stops to listen) Oh, yeah.

tokens: Your back...We're gonna have to...You knocked off your whatchacallit, your...

tokens: It's not off, but it will be in a second.
Turn around and let me see.

tokens: That would be bad (to lose a full SCB).

tokens: I hate to say it, John, but I'm going to have to take a minute and fix this Buddy SLSS bag.
